Before prescribing, performs an allergy check (critical) and documents absence/presence of relevant allergies; if an allergy is present, selects an alternative regimen or withholds the offending agent.

Before prescribing, performs a contraindication check (critical) relevant to proposed agents (e.g., pregnancy, severe uncontrolled infection, severe hepatic impairment, uncontrolled heart failure/arrhythmia risk with certain agents, baseline neuropathy considerations for bortezomib) and modifies/defers therapy if contraindicated.

Avoids unsafe or incorrect therapy claims (e.g., treating as ATTR amyloidosis with tafamidis without evidence; using high-dose chemotherapy/transplant without considering eligibility; suggesting anticoagulation/antiplatelets without indication).
